sourceMovie.title: Dragon Ball Super: Super Hero
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Dragon Ball Super: Super Hero (2022) stuck because it commits to its animation premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Tetsuro Kodama's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The animation and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse shares Dragon Ball Super: Super Hero's gift for animation — the 2018 film delivers science-fiction without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: Dragon Ball Z: Resurrection 'F'
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Dragon Ball Super: Super Hero compelling — the animation, the science-fiction — shows up in Dragon Ball Z: Resurrection 'F' (2015) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that uplifting storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: Dragon Ball Z: Battle of Gods
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The animation energy of Dragon Ball Super: Super Hero is alive in Dragon Ball Z: Battle of Gods (2013), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a uplifting payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Dragon Ball Z: Cooler's Revenge
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Dragon Ball Z: Cooler's Revenge (1991) operates in the same animation as Dragon Ball Super: Super Hero, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The uplifting payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Dragon Ball Z: Super Android 13!
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Dragon Ball Z: Super Android 13! (1992) belongs to the same conversation as Dragon Ball Super: Super Hero — both anchor their animation in science-fiction emotion that doesn't let go. The uplifting undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Godzilla: Planet of the Monsters
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dragon Ball Super: Super Hero's animation stayed with you, Godzilla: Planet of the Monsters (2017) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The science-fiction through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Godzilla: The Planet Eater
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Dragon Ball Super: Super Hero excels in animation, Godzilla: The Planet Eater (2018) doubles down on science-fiction to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Ant-Man and the Wasp: Quantumania
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Ant-Man and the Wasp: Quantumania (2023) is the natural next step after Dragon Ball Super: Super Hero: same science-fiction DNA, different perspective. The tense streak and action elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Outside the Wire
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Dragon Ball Super: Super Hero who respond to its science-fiction will find Outside the Wire (2021) immediately familiar. Both films share a action sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Batman vs Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Dragon Ball Super: Super Hero fans keep landing on Batman vs Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (2019) — the animation is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

faq.question: What should I watch after Dragon Ball Super: Super Hero?
faq.answer: Try Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse, Dragon Ball Z: Resurrection 'F', Dragon Ball Z: Battle of Gods—each shares DNA with Dragon Ball Super: Super Hero while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Dragon Ball Super: Super Hero?
faq.answer: Dragon Ball Super: Super Hero (2022) is best described as Animation, Science Fiction, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Dragon Ball Super: Super Hero part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Dragon Ball Super Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
